title: Search and Filters Across Boards
excerpt: In my Workspace I have 13 unique boards. I want to filter all 13 boards for all the board Items under the Group “New Ideas”,  and then sort these by Create Date. Any idea how to do this? I do not want to see results from…

posts.author: mBuck (Matthew)
posts.cookedHtml: In my Workspace I have 13 unique boards. I want to filter all 13 boards for all the board Items under the Group “New Ideas”, and then sort these by Create Date. Any idea how to do this? I do not want to see results from Workspaces of my colleagues. Thanks

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are they all assigned to you? Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: mBuck (Matthew)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Desiree

Thank you for your interest.

I am using work management

The version is Pro.

In the office there are about 15 people with monday setups - the set ups are essentially the same. I have 13 boards in my workspace. Typically in the work flow items representing tasks are created, and these items/tasks are completed. ON any given day maybe 15 new items are created across the 13 boards. At any one time across the 13 boards there may be a total of 300 items - some recent, some old. And so what would be good is to be able to filer across 13 boards (300 items) and see as one list the most recent items, say the last two weeks.

I hope that helps.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it! I’d recommend using a dashboard with all of those boards connected to it. Then you’ll be able to see all the tasks in one place.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com
